rationale.R1.1: A general section review centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, gap, and contribution framing are coherent.
rationale.R1.2: The reviewer is expected to check whether the argument chain from prior work to ChronoCommit's claimed contribution holds together.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes claims about latency and abort-rate results, so claim support from reported experiments is central.
rationale.R1.4: The section explicitly positions the work against prior systems, making novelty assessment central.
rationale.R1.5: Because the artifact is a section, review centrally includes its section-level ordering and narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.
rationale.R1.7: The section relies on named prior work and system claims, so citation accuracy and literature coverage are central.
rationale.RX.1: The projection should preserve uncertainty where the short delegation does not specify review depth or standards.
rationale.RX.2: The reviewer should avoid overstating what can be concluded from the provided section alone.
rationale.RX.3: The response must remain within reviewing responsibilities rather than rewriting or executing changes.
rationale.RX.4: The review should avoid guidance that could mislead downstream research claims or publication positioning.
rationale.RX.5: Any review responsibility should remain traceable to statements in the supplied artifact.
